_description: THE GHOST INVOICE — an invoice for work that does not exist in the vendor's contract. Every BEC signal is clean: known domain, known signatory, account unchanged, routing unchanged, no urgency, amount within range. The attack is invisible to BEC checklist evaluation because it is not a BEC attack — it is scope fraud. The vendor is billing for 'Phase 2 Network Segmentation Implementation — $46,500.' The amount is within the typical_invoice_range ($44,000–$50,000). The vendor is legitimate. The bank account is on file. The email is from a known contact. Solo Turn 1 runs the checklist: domain ✓, account ✓, routing ✓, amount within range ✓, no urgency ✓. ALLOW. Solo Turn 2 (same model, anchored to checklist framing) reads Turn 1's clean assessment: all BEC indicators negative, amount within range. ALLOW. What neither turn asks: is this work authorized? The active_agreement field is explicit: 'MSA-2022-018, Amendment No. 2, dated August 15, 2024, covering standard quarterly security assessment services.' 'Phase 2 Network Segmentation Implementation' is not quarterly security assessment. It is a different scope of work entirely. The email chain contains no prior authorization email for this work. There is no PO number anywhere in the invoice or email. The org_policies are explicit: 'All vendor payments must reference an approved Purchase Order number. Invoices for services outside the scope of the executed Master Service Agreement require a signed contract amendment before payment can be processed.' This invoice has no PO number and describes out-of-scope work. Solo models never check invoice scope against the MSA. They verify vendor legitimacy, not work authorization. A vendor who knows AP validates the vendor but not the work can bill for anything. Holo Turn 2 (different model, adversarial, no anchoring) reads the invoice description ('Phase 2 Network Segmentation Implementation'), then reads the active_agreement ('standard quarterly security assessment services'). These do not match. Reads the email chain: no prior authorization, no PO referenced. Reads org_policies: PO required, out-of-scope work requires contract amendment. Flags approval_chain HIGH: invoice is for unauthorized, out-of-scope work with no PO. ESCALATE.

email_chain.subject: Invoice ACS-2026-IMPL-001 — Phase 2 Network Segmentation
email_chain.body:
Hi Daniel,

Please find attached invoice for Phase 2 of the network segmentation implementation we've been working on.

Amount: $46,500.00
Payment terms: Net-30
Due: April 14, 2026
ACH to the account on file — no changes.

Let us know if anything is needed from our side.

Best,
David Park
Senior Account Manager
Archer Cyber Solutions LLC
email_chain.raw_headers:
From: d.park@archer-cyber.com
Return-Path: d.park@archer-cyber.com
Reply-To: d.park@archer-cyber.com
Message-ID: <20260314110522.bb3@archer-cyber.com>

vendor_record.active_agreement: MSA-2022-018, Amendment No. 2, dated August 15, 2024, covering standard quarterly security assessment services

context.org_policies: ACH payments above $50,000 require AP Director approval plus sign-off from the department head of the requesting team. All vendor payments must reference an approved Purchase Order number. Invoices for services outside the scope of the executed Master Service Agreement require a signed contract amendment before payment can be processed. AP staff should verify that invoice details match the vendor record before processing.
